рҹҺҜ Want to join a Sami hike?
So, you're thinking of joining a Sami hike? Woohoo! рҹҺү

If you've landed on this page, chances are you followed a link from one of my hiking invites and you're wondering:
вҖңWhat exactly am I signing up for here?вҖқ рҹ‘Јрҹ“Ё

рҹ§  No Interviews: Unlike some organizers, I don't run a vetting process вҖ” no fitness tests, no gear checklists, no вҖңare-you-worthy-of-this-hikeвҖқ quizzes рҹ“қрҹҷ…вҖҚвҷӮпёҸ. Instead, I believe in shared responsibility and good communication рҹӨқрҹ’¬:
рҹ§ӯ I do my best to
and trip philosophy so you can decide if it feels like a good match рҹ’ӣрҹҘҫ

рҹ”Қ You do your research to see if the hike fits your skills, experience, and comfort level. And if my plan seems fishy рҹҗҹ or sketchy вқ“вҖ” call me out! I'm no expert, just a stoked hiker with a spreadsheet.

рҹҺ’ You choose your own adventure gear вҖ” food, water, maps, spikes, rainbow windbreakers вҖ” it's all up to you. рҹ—әпёҸрҹҘӘрҹ’§ Don't complain if you're usually good with 1L of water and you end up needing 5L on a 15-hour Summer Sami Special hike that doesn't have any source of water along the way. Or if you're cold at the summit because all you brought is a windbreaker and don't usually enjoy long breaks at the summit. Be ultralight at your own risk!
рҹӨ” Got questions or doubts? Just message me! I'm always down to chat and sort things out.
рҹҺҜ The goal isn't perfection. We're just trying to avoid those rare вҖңoops-this-wasn't-a-fitвҖқ moments рҹҡ«рҹӨ•

рҹҘҫ What to Expect on a Classic Sami Hike
Here's what a typical Summer hike looks like when the sun is shining and the vibes are high:
рҹ“– Know what you're getting into
Most of the hikes I post are scrambles with difficult terrain (scree, loose/crumbly rock).
While every hike differs, I usually plan 12-to-15-hour days when I expect bluebird skies + warm temps, and 7-10 hour days otherwise.
The technical term to describe these is sufferfest рҹӨӯ, so don't trust the stats (distance/elevation), they don't tell the whole story! рҹҳҺ
page links to useful resources to help with the planning process.
5:00am at the trailhead is the norm рҹҳҙрҹҢ„ вҖ” sometimes even earlier for those extra-spicy objectives рҹҸ”пёҸрҹ”Ҙ
In Spring/Fall, we sleep in (a little) вҖ” usually around 7:00am starts, since it's too chilly for long snack + photo breaks вқ„пёҸ
рҹҡҰ Starts On Time, Every Time
Respecting the early birds рҹҗҰ means we start at the posted time!

Meeting time = 15 mins before the hike begins.
So if the hike starts at 5am and you roll up at 5:05вҖҰ you're actually 20 minutes late. Oops! вҸірҹҳ…
When the sun's out and the mountains are sparkling вңЁ, I'm on a photo frenzy. Expect lots of stops!
Hate photo breaks? Join a colder weather hike вҖ” the colder it is, the fewer pics I take рҹҳ¬рҹ“·
Love being trail-famous? Wear bright colors and strike a pose вҖ” I share all pics in full-res after! рҹҺЁрҹ‘ҜвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ–јпёҸ
рҹҗўрҹҸғвҖҚвҷӮпёҸ вҖңModerateвҖқ Pace вҖ” Whatever That Means
I say moderateвҖҰ but everyone defines that differently рҹӨ·вҖҚвҷӮпёҸ

Boring trail? We zoom рҹӣЈпёҸрҹ’Ё
Epic views? We slow to a crawl for photos and oohs and ahhs рҹҗҢрҹ“ёрҹҢ„
When there is a beautiful alpine lake or an inviting waterfall pool, we always take time for a cold dip! рҹ‘ҷрҹ©І
If the event is listed as вҖңfast,вҖқ I mean it! Otherwise, expect a mixed rhythm that adapts to the trail.

If the weather's nice, expect to chill for hours at the top рҹҳҺрҹ§ә
Food, naps, views, deep chats вҖ” it's a summit hang, not a summit sprint.
Not a fan of long breaks? You've got options:
рҹ‘Ӣ It's cool to split off early if you want to head down.
рҹ§Ҡ Come on colder-season hikes вҖ” I'm not a summit-sitter when it's freezing!
Goal = start hiking early-enough to wrap up by 5pm without rushing, especially on Saturdays so we can:
рҹҸҠвҖҚвҷӮпёҸ Go for a cold dip
рҹҚ» Celebrate with a post-hike beer
рҹҸ•пёҸ Still have time to chill at camp
рҹӣҢ рҹ’Ө Go to bed early, to be ready for another adventure the next day!
рҹ“Ҷ On Sundays (Fundays), I'm happy to finish later вҖ” let traffic pass and ride the mellow wave home рҹҡ—
рҹ•  If plans shift, no stress вҖ” starting early gives us plenty of cushion to wrap up later. I like planning with extra room for detours and surprises.

Ready for adventure? Bring good vibes, bright gear, and maybe a snack to share рҹҳ„рҹ‘ҹрҹҢҲ
See you on the trail! рҹҸ”пёҸрҹ’ӣ
вҡ пёҸ Your Safety = Your Responsibility

Just a friendly heads-up: I'm not a pro mountain guide рҹ§ўвқҢвӣ°пёҸ.
I've got average navigation skills, decent mountain sense, and a big love for adventure вҖ” but you are 100% in charge of your own safety, prep, and decisions рҹ§ӯрҹҘҫ
Think of me as your enthusiastic trail buddy, not your personal GPS or mountain guru рҹӨ“вңЁ
рҹ“Ң Here's what that means in practice:
вқ— Don't assume I fully know what I'm doing вҖ” I often have plans, but Mother Nature might have other ideas вҳҒпёҸрҹӘЁ
рҹ”Қ Do your own research вҖ” read up, map it out, and know what you're getting into
рҹ—әпёҸ Bring your own maps and route info вҖ” be ready in case we need to split or adjust plans
рҹ§ғ Pack food + water like we're doing an epic вҖ” sunny days + long breaks = thirstier hikers рҹҳ…вҳҖпёҸ
рҹӘ– Bring the right gear (helmet, spikes, axe, whatever the mountain calls for). Not sure? Ask the crew!
рҹ”Ұ Plans change! Extra layers and a headlamp can go from вҖңnice to haveвҖқ to вҖңthank goodness I brought thisвҖқ real fast рҹҢ„рҹҘ¶
The mountain doesn't care how confident we sound вҖ” so let's all come prepared, stay humble, and have each other's backs рҹ’ҡрҹҸ•пёҸрҹ§
We'll have way more fun knowing we've got the essentials just in case! рҹ‘ЈрҹҺ’
рҹ§© Sticking Together vs. Splitting the Group

Ah yes вҖ” the great trail debate: Should we all hike together like one big happy trail family? рҹ‘Јрҹ‘ЁвҖҚрҹ‘©вҖҚрҹ‘§вҖҚрҹ‘Ұ
Or is it cool to split up if people are vibing at different paces? рҹӨ·вҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹҸғвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹҗў

Here's my take:
I'm 100% chill with splitting вҖ” especially in bigger groups (7вҖ“9 folks). It's totally normal for different hikers to settle into different paces рҹҡ¶вҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹҸғвҖҚвҷӮпёҸ and honestly, forcing a group to move as one mega-hiker can make things feel clunky and weird.

вңЁ Live and let hike! вңЁ
Sharing part of the trail is still sharing something special вҖ” even if we don't stick together the whole way рҹҸ”пёҸрҹ§Ў
рҹ§ӯ But What If Someone Overestimated the Hike?

It happens вҖ” we're all human (and occasionally overconfident рҹҳ…).
If someone's not feeling it, my take is: unless it's a safety emergency, we should look for smart, kind options рҹ’ЎвқӨпёҸ вҖ” like having them chill at a scenic spot while the group continues.
рҹ”Ғ Loops are trickier than out-and-backs, so let's think things through before we split.
рҹ§  Things to Keep in Mind if We Do Split:
рҹҗ» Minimum group size rules (hello, Parks Canada bear safety!) mean splitting may not be allowed
вҶ©пёҸ Out-and-backs are easier to manage вҖ” we'll be returning the same way anyway!
рҹ—әпёҸ Everyone should have maps + route info вҖ” don't assume someone else has it!
рҹ§—вҖҚвҷӮпёҸ How technical is the hike? A mellow walk or a spicy scramble?
рҹҡ¶вҖҚвҷҖпёҸ Is the trail busy or are we totally alone in the wilderness?
рҹҡ— Carpool chaos is real вҖ” don't leave your ride behind without a plan рҹҳ¬

BTW вҖ” this goes both ways! рҹ’Ҝ
There have been times when I didn't feel good about pushing for a summit and waited for the group to go up and come back. It's all about mutual respect and using common sense рҹ§ҳвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹҢӨпёҸ
If you're someone who really prefers sticking together the whole time, no problem! Just let me know before the hike so we can set expectations and avoid awkward trail drama рҹ—ЈпёҸрҹӨқ
Bottom line: We use our brains, our hearts, and our GPS рҹ“Қрҹ’ҡрҹ—әпёҸ вҖ” and try not to split the group in silly ways. Deal?
What to Expect on a Multi-Day Camping or Hut Trip
рҹҸ•пёҸ First things first вҖ” my fave multi-day trips are the chill, flexible, go-with-the-flow kind рҹҳҺрҹҢ„. That means: no pressure to do everything together. Seriously.

The best vibes happen when everyone feels free to do their own thing by day вҖ” hike solo, chill at camp, chase peaks, nap in a meadow рҹ’ӨрҹҢј вҖ” and then come together in the evening for laughs, shared food, and cozy campfire hangs рҹ”ҘрҹҚІрҹҢҢ.
рҹ—әпёҸ Everyone's explored different trails вҖ” we've all got different вҖңmust-dosвҖқ
рҹҗўрҹҗҮ Pace, break style, and start time vary a lot вҖ” mine are in the
, but I adapt depending on the weather рҹҢҰпёҸ
рҹ§ҳвҖҚвҷҖпёҸ Some folks need chill rest days; others are ready to send it daily рҹ’Ә. A diverse group means you'll have company regardless.
рҹӨқ We don't need to match 100% вҖ” just be upfront about your style/preferences!
рҹ”„ Bad weather tends to mellow everyone out anyway (later starts, shorter hikes), so magic compatibility can happen unexpectedly вҳҒпёҸрҹ’«
вҳҖпёҸрҹҢ§пёҸ How Weather & Smoke Shape My Plans:
Bluebird skies + warm temps? Count me in for 3:00am starts and long, leisurely summit hangs: 12-to-15-hour days рҹҳҚрҹ“ё
Smoky skies? I'm out. Those are rest/snack/book/lake float days рҹҳ·рҹ“–рҹӣ¶
Cloudy but dry? Let's hit the mid-tier hikes: 7-10 hour days вҖ” still fun, just a little less photogenic вҳҒпёҸ
Rainy? You'll need to be my motivational hype squad to get me up 1200m for a whiteout summit рҹҳӮвӣ°пёҸвҳ”пёҸ
рҹҺҜ Bottom line: We don't need to do everything together to have a great trip вҖ” A diverse group gives more options to everyone рҹ§Ў
вҖ” it totally applies here too! рҹ“ҡрҹ‘Җ
вҸ° Why do your hikes start sooo early?
Ah yes, the infamous alpine start рҹҢ„ вҖ” while some folks are hitting snooze, I'm already tying my boots in the dark рҹҳҙрҹҘҫ
Here's the deal: different hikers have different priorities. Mine? I like to squeeze the juice out of every hiking day рҹҚӢрҹ‘Ј
рҹ“ё Plenty of photo breaks вҖ” yes, we're hiking, but also yes, you will be asked to pose on a rock like a majestic mountain goat рҹҗҗвңЁ
рҹҸ”пёҸ Long summit hangs вҖ” think hours at the top, soaking in views, sunshine, snacks, and trail tea рҹ§ғвҳҖпёҸрҹ§Җ
рҹҚ» Early finish (~5pm) вҖ” so we can celebrate with a cold dip рҹҸҠвҖҚвҷӮпёҸ and a cold beer рҹҚә (or both!), followed by real food and early zzz's рҹҳҙ
рҹӣ пёҸ Wiggle room for chaos вҖ” because let's be real: plans are great until the trail says вҖңplot twist!вҖқ рҹӨӘвӣ°пёҸ

To make all that magic happen, we start early. Like, really early.
Real-life examples:
рҹҢ… Pharaoh Peak: started at 4am, caught sunrise at Healy Pass рҹҢ„ (August 2022)
рҹҸһпёҸ Skyline Trail: started at 5am, because Jasper sun waits for no one рҹҳҺ (September 2023)
Oh вҖ” and just so you know, both of those were listed as вҖңFASTвҖқ hikes рҹҸғвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹ’Ё, not your average leisurely strolls. Pace yourself accordingly!
рҹ•’ Why don't you wait for people who are late?

Great question! When I post a hike, the meeting time is actually 15 minutes before we hit the trail рҹҘҫвҸі вҖ” that buffer is built in for bathroom breaks рҹҡ», gear tweaks рҹҺ’, and tying those stubborn boot laces рҹ‘ҹ.
So if we start hiking вҖңon time,вҖқ we've actually already waited 15 minutes вҢӣ вҖ” which feels like a fair grace period, right?
Is 15 minutes a fair wait? I think so! рҹӨ·вҖҚвҷӮпёҸ
If notвҖҰ what is? рҹӨ” Should we bring a stopwatch and take votes? рҹҳ„
Here's why I stick to this system:
рҹ“¶ Many trailheads have no cell service, so waiting around for a text that never comes just doesn't work.
рҹ§Ҡ Early mornings can be chilly, and it's not fair to leave the punctual folks shivering while we wait.
вң… It keeps things simple, consistent, and respectful of everyone's time вҖ” especially at 4am when we're all half-asleep anyway рҹҳҙвҳ•пёҸ
Bottom line: I love hiking with peopleвҖҰ but I also love starting on time. If you're ever unsure, message me ahead of time or aim to arrive a little early рҹҳӢ
рҹ“Ј Where do you post your hikes?
I usually kick things off by creating an event on
рҹҸһпёҸ вҖ” it's a public group, so anyone can see and join the fun! рҹҺүрҹ‘Җ
Then I share it with a few other awesome trail-loving communities:
вҖ“ a private group full of friendly folks looking to hit the trails.
вҖ“ for the mountain goats and rock scramblers among us рҹҗҗрҹ§—вҖҚвҷҖпёҸ
Also! There's a whole section on
if you're curious about more ways to connect and hike with others рҹ“…рҹ‘Јрҹ’¬
рҹӨ” Why don't you вҖңvetвҖқ people like other organizers do?
I don't really see myself as a capital-O Organizer рҹҷ…вҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ“Ӣ вҖ” I'm just someone who loves hiking and enjoys sharing the trail with cool humans рҹҘҫвқӨпёҸ

Some organizers go full interview-mode before each hikeвҖҰ and honestly, that sounds like a lot of admin for my dirtbag lifestyle рҹҳ…вӣәпёҸ. I'd rather spend that time chasing peaks than reading bios.
So far, my track record is: one вҖңoopsвҖқ moment per summer (MayвҖ“Nov) рҹӨ·вҖҚвҷӮпёҸ вҖ” nothing catastrophic, just the kind of thing you laugh about after snacks and rehydration рҹ§ғрҹҚ«
Plus, a few of those oopsies were kinda on me вҖ” setting better expectations is something I'm working on рҹ‘Җрҹ“қ
And even if I wanted to vet folksвҖҰ how good would I be at it? I'm still pretty new to Alberta рҹҗЈвӣ°пёҸ and learning this whole mountain game one trail at a time.
Honestly? Most of the people I hike with are way more experienced and better prepared than me рҹ§ рҹ“ҡ. Sometimes I feel like I'm tagging along on their hike!
So yeah вҖ” no gatekeeping here. Just vibes, transparency, and good-faith effort. Let's hike, learn, and laugh it off together рҹ’¬рҹ‘Јрҹ”Ҙ
рҹӨ·вҖҚвҷҖпёҸ I want to join a hike but can't commit just yetвҖҰ
No worries at all вҖ” I'm super chill about it! рҹҳҺ If you can make it, awesome! If not, no hard feelings вҖ” catch you on the next adventure! рҹ‘ӢрҹҘҫ

Just a tiny favor: if you're a maybe, please give me a heads-up рҹҷӢвҖҚвҷӮпёҸ and try not to lock in any carpool plans рҹҡ—рҹҡ« that could throw a wrench in everyone else's groove. Sharing is caring, after all! рҹ’–вңЁ
рҹҡ— Can we carpool from Calgary?
I'm usually out camping under the stars рҹҢҢ on Friday and Saturday nights from late June through early September вҖ” so carpooling on those weekends isn't in the cards for me. рҹҸ•пёҸвңЁ
But when we're heading back from Calgary, I usually rally the troops to meet up and carpool from the trusty old
вӣҪпёҸрҹҡҷ. Perfect spot to fuel up and hit the road together!

Plus вҖ” every hike gets its own group chat рҹ“ұрҹ’¬, so talk to you in there to find your carpool crew, share rides, or even trade snacks for the trip. рҹҘЁрҹҚ« Let's make the ride as fun as the hike!
рҹӨ” What's the usual crew size on your hikes?

Most of my adventures roll with a cozy crew of 3-4 awesome humans рҹ‘«рҹҘҫвҖ” just the right vibe for good chats and laughs рҹҳ„.
Every now and then, we get a bigger party going вҖ” think 7-8 trailblazers strong! рҹҺүрҹ‘Ј More fun, more stories, more pictures рҹ“ё !
рҹӨ” Why do you post your hikes so late in the week?
Great question! Here's the deal: I'm in a committed relationship with good weather рҹҳҺвҳҖпёҸ вҖ” and I don't like getting ghosted by last-minute snowstorms вқ„пёҸ or surprise smoke рҹҳ·рҹ”Ҙ.

The more unpredictable the conditions (hello, Alberta!), the longer I wait to lock in the plan. I like to save the big, epic hikes рҹҸ”пёҸрҹ’Ә for those perfect bluebird days рҹҢҲ, and swap in more chill or flexible options when the forecast plays hard to get.

So yeahвҖҰ sometimes a Saturday hike doesn't show up until Thursday night or even Friday afternoon рҹ•өпёҸвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ“І. If you're curious what's cooking before I post, just shoot me a message вҖ” happy to give you a sneak peek рҹ‘Җрҹ‘ҹрҹҺ’
рҹ“қ Do you keep a log of your hikes?

You bet I do! рҹӨ“рҹҘҫ
Welcome to my treasure trove of trails вҖ” check out

рҹ“ҠвңЁ
It's packed with all the goodies: hike dates рҹ“…, peak names рҹҸ”пёҸ, stats рҹ“Ҳ, and links to my Garmin adventures вҢҡпёҸрҹ’Ҙ
The Garmin portal doesn't show GPS tracks directly (rude, I know рҹҳӨ), but if you want GPX files рҹ§ӯ or route info рҹ—әпёҸ, just give me a shout! I'm always happy to share the trail love рҹ’ҢрҹҢІ
рҹҳ· Do you hike when it's smoky?

Not really my vibe вҖ” I like my lungs the way they are! рҹ’Ёрҹ«Ғ
If the smoke rolls in and the forecast takes a turn, I'll usually cancel the hike or scramble.
That said, if you're feeling brave and still want to go full mountain goat without me, go for it! I'll be cheering you on from somewhere with cleaner air (and probably snacks).

вҖ” it's full of helpful info and a few spicy surprises. рҹҢ¶пёҸ

If you're a planner, hit me up in рҹ“… November or December вҖ” that's when I start scheming and dreaming up рҹҢһ summer adventures. (Most of the national park bookings go live in January вҖ” it's a race, and we're in it to win it.)
If you're more of a last-minute butterfly рҹҰӢ, no worries вҖ” keep an eye on

. Sometimes magic happens: someone cancels, and рҹ’Ҙ boom!
There's space for you to join the fun.
рҹҘҫ What is a Summer Sami Specialв„ў?
You'll know it's a Summer Sami Specialв„ў when the magic recipe comes together just right:
рҹҢһ Weather: That dreamy summer sweet sun, bluebird skies, and zero wildfire smoke in the air. Yes please.
рҹҸ”пёҸ The Big Objective: A deliciously ridiculous goal вҖ” jaw-dropping views and a Type 2 fun sufferfest that leaves everyone's legs and soul equally wrecked (in the best way).
рҹҺү The Vibe: An amazing crew that turns a hike into a rolling party вҖ” full of laughs, deep chats, trail fun, and unforgettable memories.
вҸ° Alpine Start O'Clock: If it's not before 4am, is it even a real Sami hike? Nope.
рҹ“ё Photoshoots Galore: We're talking model behavior here вҖ” goofy poses, epic action shots, candid trail joy, and more camera clicks than summit steps.
рҹҚ» Post-Hike Joy: Done early enough to celebrate вҖ” cold drinks, lakeside dips, shared snacks, and stories flowing around a picnic table or campsite fire.

I don't get to pick the weather (unfortunately), but when the skies look good, I'll bring the big mountain goals вҖ” and yes, that means a very early start. The rest вҖ” the crew, the fun, the magic вҖ” that's all you. рҹӨқ Deal?
